wizard_page.title: WizardScape
wizard_page.subtitle: A 3D mobile survival prototype featuring auto-cast combat and dynamic power-up systems.

about.title: Survivor-like Mechanics
desc: WizardScape is a technical prototype developed to explore the 'Reverse Bullet Hell' genre on mobile devices using Unreal Engine 5.4.
The project focuses on the core pillars of the genre: autonomous combat logic, high-density enemy spawning, and a data-driven meta-progression shop.
As a Pre-Alpha demo, it served as a playground for optimizing C++ performance on Android while managing real-time stat modifiers.

gameplay.title: Survival Loop
items: • Auto-Cast Combat: A circular proximity system that automatically targets and triggers spells against the closest threats.
• Diamond Ecosystem: Multi-tiered resource collection: Blue (Currency), Green (Temporary Boosts), and Red (Challenge Modifiers).
• Meta-Progression: A persistent shop system where players can spend collected resources to permanently upgrade wizard stats.
• Dynamic Swarm: Procedural wave spawning with increasing enemy density and scaling difficulty over time.

tech.title: Mobile Systems
items: • Optimized Spawning: A C++ component-based spawner (Ref: Commit f1620027) designed to handle high enemy counts efficiently.
• Modifier Architecture: Flexible buff/debuff logic (Ref: Commit aed74a58) allowing gems to inject temporary or permanent stat changes.
• Android Pipeline: Full integration with the modern Android SDK/NDK stack and customized Java setup for UE 5.4.
• Collision Filtering: Specialized physics channels to manage interactions between the wizard, gems, and enemy mages.

systems.title: UX & Performance
items: • Mobile-First HUD: Vertical UI layout optimized for one-handed play, featuring integrated experience bars and status icons.
• Virtual Joystick: Custom-tuned touch input mapping to ensure fluid movement in high-pressure combat scenarios.
• Asset Management: Systematic cleanup of unused resources and texture optimization to maintain stability on mobile hardware.

spawner.title: Procedural Swarm Logic
spawner.desc: Implementation of a high-performance C++ spawner. It manages enemy mage pools and handles the 'Dead Gem' offset logic to ensure resources drop correctly upon enemy defeat.
spawner.img1: Spawner Configuration: Details of the spawn frequency and enemy density scaling settings.

gems.title: Data-Driven Modifiers
gems.desc: Analysis of the Buff/Debuff system. Each gem type (Blue, Green, Red) interacts with the wizard's base stats via a centralized modifier component.
gems.img1: Gem Logic: View of the resource actors and their respective trigger events for stat injection.

shop.title: Meta-Progression Shop
shop.desc: A functional upgrade interface parented to a C++ class. It handles the serialization of player diamonds and tracks permanent attribute improvements.
shop.img1: Upgrade Interface: The shop UI layout showing stat-buy logic and cost scaling.

input.title: Touch & Virtual Joystick
input.desc: Custom integration of the UE Virtual Joystick. The input mapping was specifically tuned (Ref: Commit 2c24f4e1) to balance precision and responsiveness on Android.
input.img1: Mobile Input Setup: Configuration of the touch interface and joystick sensitivity.

pipeline.title: Android SDK/NDK Setup
pipeline.desc: Documentation of the migration to UE 5.4, including the updated Java and Android requirement configurations for modern mobile distribution.
pipeline.img1: Platform Settings: A look at the Project Settings for Android builds and icon packaging.

ui.title: Vertical HUD Design
ui.desc: A UI architecture designed for portrait mode. It centralizes real-time info like diamond count and timer, using C++ classes for optimized widget logic.
ui.img1: Mobile UX: Showcase of the in-game HUD and the integrated tutorial system.

cta.title: Ready to survive?
cta.subtitle: Download the Pre-Alpha APK and test the core mechanics on Android.
